name: United Quest Card

welcome_offer.description: Earn 90,000 bonus miles after you spend $4,000 on purchases in the first 3 months your account is open. Plus earn 3,000 PQP (Premier Qualifying Points) after spending $4,000. Additional 10,000 bonus miles if you add an authorized user in first 3 months.

earning_rates.category: United purchases (airline tickets, seat upgrades, Economy Plus, inflight food/beverages/Wi-Fi, United fees)
earning_rates.notes: Earned on United purchases made directly with United

earning_rates.category: Dining at restaurants (including takeout and eligible delivery services)

earning_rates.notes: Excludes United purchases that qualify for 4 miles

earning_rates.category: Renowned Hotels and Resorts via Chase Travel
earning_rates.notes: Prepaid hotel accommodations booked through Chase Travel with Renowned Hotels and Resorts

annual_credits.name: United TravelBank Cash
annual_credits.description: $200 annually in TravelBank cash for United- or United Express-operated flights

annual_credits.name: Renowned Hotels and Resorts Credit
annual_credits.description: Up to $150 in statement credits per anniversary year when booking prepaid stays through Renowned Hotels and Resorts program via Chase Travel

other_benefits: 2 free checked bags for you and one companion on same reservation on United-operated flights
10,000-mile discount on award flights each year after first cardmember anniversary
25% back on inflight purchases (food, beverages, Wi-Fi) as statement credit
Global Entry or TSA PreCheck credit up to $120 every 4 years
Primary rental car coverage (up to $60,000 for theft/collision damage)
Trip delay insurance (up to $500 for expenses after 12-hour delay)
Delayed baggage insurance (up to $100/day for 3 days)
Complimentary DashPass membership for 1 year (activate by 12/31/2027)

sources.notes: Chase official United Quest page with 90,000 mile welcome bonus and earning rates
NerdWallet article on United Quest benefits including $350 annual fee, $200 TravelBank credit, and 4X United earning
The Points Guy on 2025 United card overhaul: annual fee increased from $250 to $350

notes: Annual fee increased from $250 to $350 in March 2025. The $200 annual TravelBank credit significantly offsets the $350 annual fee for United flyers. The $150 Renowned Hotels credit and complimentary Premier Silver status add additional value. With 4X on United purchases and 2X on dining/streaming/other travel, frequent United travelers can earn substantial miles. The 10,000-mile award flight discount each year after the first anniversary is valuable for award bookings. PQP earning (1 per $20 spent, up to 18,000/year) helps toward Premier status. Note: The $0 annual fee United Gateway card may be better for those who don't fly United frequently.
